title: Introduction
description: NOC 64312 – Airline ticket and service agents | GTR Immigration NOC 64312 (Airline ticket and service agents) professionals are the operational heart of Canada's aviation industry. From issuing tickets and managing check-ins to the critical calculation of aircraft load weights, these TEER 4 agents ensure the safety and efficiency of air travel. With Canada's major airports in Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal seeing record-breaking passenger volumes in 2026, the demand for skilled agents is at an all-time high. For immigration, this role offers a stable entry into the transportation sector with significant growth potential into supervisory and load planning management.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in food, accommodation and tourismMinor Group6431 – Occupations in travel and accommodationNOC Code64312

duties: Issue tickets and handle passenger baggage at check-in counters.
Assist passengers at the gate during boarding and deplaning.
Calculate aircraft weight and balance and prepare load sheets for pilots (Load Planners).
Provide information on flight schedules, fares, and gate assignments.
Trace lost or misdirected baggage and process claims for damaged items.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Several weeks of on-the-job training are provided by the employer.
Load planners usually require specialized certification from the airline.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. Over 12,000 new job openings are expected due to the recovery of international travel and the expansion of domestic low-cost carriers.
labour_market_demand: The demand for airline ticket and service agents is currently Good, especially in provinces with major international hubs (Ontario, BC, and Alberta). As airlines expand their global routes, there is a specific shortage of Load Planners and agents with multilingual capabilities to assist international travelers.
Aviation Growth: Work with global leaders like Air Canada and WestJet during a period of massive industry expansion.
Specialized Training: Gain high-level technical skills in aircraft weight/balance and global distribution systems (GDS).
Travel Benefits: Access to flight "standby" perks and industry discounts across the global aviation network.
pathways: CEC (Canadian Experience Class): Accessible for agents with 12 months of Canadian work experience.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Targeted draws for the transportation sector.
Saskatchewan INP: Addressing regional airport operational needs.
